note: First clean container run after the Tor-rotation fix. Hit one Imperva doodle captcha, solved it on attempt 1, one Tor exit rotation. Diffed against the previous run: 40/40 products identical — catalog stable, no price/stock changes.
Local verification run. Drew a clean Tor exit on the first circuit — zero captcha walls, so the solver never had to fire. Proved the extraction + parse + report pipeline end to end.

First container run — surfaced a real bug. The Dockerfile baked a Tor control-password hash that did not match the plaintext the app sends, so every NEWNYM exit-rotation failed auth (515). The container got stuck on a flagged Tor exit it could never leave, looping on captcha walls the overloaded free vision providers (Gemini 503, Groq 429) could not clear. Fixed: hash now generated at build time from the real password, rotateExit() verifies the 250 OK reply, and the container self-checks auth at boot. Next run (above) was clean.
errorTail:
[solve] vision: gemini-native 503 high demand -> rotate
[solve] solve: wrong/looped on attempt 3
[warm] solver gave up — rotating Tor exit
(rotation silently no-op: 515 Authentication failed)
